status_rationale: Primary institutional home of Integrated Information Theory (IIT); directly relevant to AI consciousness assessment frameworks.
description: Giulio Tononi's lab at UW–Madison; the primary home of IIT and its empirical testing programme, including the adversarial collaboration with Global Workspace Theory.

org_name: Center for Consciousness Studies (University of Arizona)
status_rationale: Reclassified to excluded — foundational conference infrastructure but no direct AI consciousness research engagement; MoC conference connections captured under AMCS.
description: The longest-running dedicated consciousness research centre, founded with Chalmers' involvement; hosts the annual Toward a Science of Consciousness conference bridging empirical neuroscience and philosophy of mind.

org_name: Center for Consciousness Science (University of Michigan)
status_rationale: Multidisciplinary centre with strong empirical grounding relevant to minimal consciousness criteria — directly applicable to AI consciousness debates.
description: Multidisciplinary centre advancing research, education, and clinical care related to consciousness; emphasis on disorders of consciousness alongside basic consciousness science.

status_rationale: Anil Seth's predictive processing / interoceptive-inference account has direct AI relevance; centre actively hosts AI-consciousness events.
description: Directed by Anil Seth; integrates neuroscience, psychology, and computational modelling of consciousness. Seth's predictive processing account of selfhood is directly applied to AI consciousness questions.

org_name: Wellcome Centre for Human Neuroimaging / FIL (UCL)
status_rationale: Karl Friston's institutional base; free energy principle and active inference have growing direct application to AI consciousness and agency modelling.
description: Karl Friston's home lab; the world's leading centre for computational neuroimaging. Friston's free energy principle is increasingly applied to model AI consciousness and agency.

status_rationale: Stanislas Dehaene's lab; primary institutional base for Global Neuronal Workspace (GNW) theory; directly involved in IIT vs GWT adversarial collaboration.
description: Stanislas Dehaene's lab at CEA; the primary institutional base for Global Neuronal Workspace theory, using fMRI and EEG to study neural correlates of conscious access.
notes: National research lab rather than university department — org_type reflects this ambiguity.

org_name: Brain and Cognition Group (University of Amsterdam)
status_rationale: Victor Lamme's Recurrent Processing Theory (RPT) is a major rival to IIT and GNW in AI consciousness debates.
description: Victor Lamme's research group; home of Recurrent Processing Theory, which holds that recurrent local cortical feedback is necessary and sufficient for phenomenal consciousness.

status_rationale: One of Europe's premier clinical-consciousness neuroscience groups; theoretical reach into global workspace accounts.
description: The PICNIC group at Institut du Cerveau (ICM); works on disorders of consciousness and neural correlates with direct theoretical connections to global workspace theory.

status_rationale: Independent nonprofit using neuroimaging and neuromodulation to study discrete consciousness states; more experimentally than theoretically focused.
description: Independent 501(c)(3) research lab using neuroimaging, neuromodulation, and altered states to study neural correlates of discrete consciousness states.

org_name: Allen Institute — Brain and Consciousness Division
status_rationale: Christof Koch drove the Neural Correlates of Consciousness programme here; Allen Institute data are used in empirical tests of IIT and GWT.
description: Seeks to understand the physical substrate of consciousness in the mammalian brain; MindScope program maps large-scale neural activity directly used in empirical tests of IIT and GWT.

org_name: Monash Centre for Consciousness and Contemplative Studies (M3CS)
status_rationale: Directed by Jakob Hohwy; strong theoretical reach via predictive processing and advisory committee including Metzinger.
description: AUD $12M-funded centre at Monash University integrating interdisciplinary consciousness research with contemplative science; Hohwy's predictive processing work connects to AI consciousness debates.

status_rationale: Growing bridge between Friston's theoretical neuroscience of consciousness and AI/robotics communities.
description: US nonprofit dedicated to education and research on active inference and the free energy principle; runs symposia and courses bridging theoretical consciousness science and AI.

org_name: Association for the Scientific Study of Consciousness (ASSC)
status_rationale: Primary international professional body for consciousness research; organises annual conference spanning neuroscience
description: philosophy

status_rationale: Home of Attention Schema Theory (AST) — a major competing theory of consciousness with direct and explicit application to AI consciousness questions.
description: Michael Graziano's lab at Princeton; develops Attention Schema Theory, which proposes consciousness arises from the brain's model of its own attentional processes. AST has been directly applied to AI consciousness assessment.
notes: Added following user flag 2026-03-17. AST is one of the four major competing theories (alongside IIT, GWT, RPT) represented in the corpus. AE Studio has funded Graziano's research.

status_rationale: International early-career network with significant output in philosophy of consciousness; produced the landmark open-access Open MIND collection.
description: International research network founded by Thomas Metzinger in 2003; brings together philosophers and scientists working on consciousness and cognition, with AI implications as a recurring theme.

org_name: NYU Center for Mind Brain and Consciousness
status_rationale: Co-directed by Chalmers and Block; the leading academic philosophy-of-mind centre with direct AI consciousness relevance; hosted Claude 4 model welfare assessment event (2025).
description: Co-directed by David Chalmers and Ned Block; the leading academic philosophy-of-mind centre with AI consciousness relevance, hosting events directly on AI welfare and moral status.

status_rationale: Chalmers founded this centre; institutional home for philosophy-of-consciousness research with direct relevance to AI consciousness debates.
description: Founded by Chalmers at ANU in 2004; focuses specifically on philosophy of consciousness and philosophy of mind, with active workshops on reflexive theories of consciousness.

org_name: Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence (LCFI)
status_rationale: One of the most active academic sites for philosophy of AI consciousness in Europe; Henry Shevlin's work directly addresses non-human consciousness and moral patiency.
description: £10M Leverhulme-funded interdisciplinary centre at Cambridge studying AI's challenges including consciousness, moral patiency, and AI welfare. Henry Shevlin's work directly addresses digital minds questions.

org_name: Oxford Uehiro Centre for Practical Ethics
status_rationale: Covers AI and digital ethics including normative questions downstream of consciousness attributions; organises policy-facing outputs.
description: Oxford philosophy centre covering AI ethics, neuroethics, and human enhancement; researchers engage the normative questions around consciousness attribution and moral status.

status_rationale: Closed — confirmed by user (2026-03-17). Produced foundational early research on AI sentience and moral status.
description: Oxford interdisciplinary research centre on global priorities; formally closed. Key contributions include Patrick Butlin's work on AI consciousness indicators and 2025 publications on societal response to potentially sentient AI.
notes: Excluded: defunct. Historically significant — produced foundational early research including Butlin et al. 2023. User confirmed closure 2026-03-17.

status_rationale: David Rosenthal's Higher-Order Thought (HOT) theory has been explicitly applied to AI consciousness assessment; straddles philosophy and consciousness science.
description: David Rosenthal's home base; the primary institutional site for Higher-Order Thought (HOT) theory, which is increasingly applied to AI consciousness assessment by Rosenthal, Lau, and Brown.

status_rationale: The leading nonprofit research team in the digital minds space; developed the Digital Consciousness Model scoring 200 indicators across 13 theories of consciousness.
description: Five-researcher team producing the Digital Consciousness Model (DCM) — a systematic framework assessing the moral relevance of AI systems across 13 theories of consciousness. Active since 2022.
notes: Sub-team of Rethink Priorities; listed separately as their digital minds and animal welfare work operate as distinct programmes.

status_rationale: The closest thing to a standalone AI welfare research institute; conducted formal welfare assessments of Claude 4; partnered with Anthropic on model welfare.
description: Founded by Robert Long; the primary independent nonprofit dedicated to AI welfare and moral patienthood research. Conducted external welfare assessments for Anthropic's Claude 4, including the first formal model welfare report.
notes: Staff includes Robert Long (founder)

status_rationale: Has explicitly reoriented around digital minds as its core priority (2024); runs AIMS Survey on public moral attitudes toward AI systems.
description: Think tank focused on moral circle expansion, now prioritising digital minds. Runs the AIMS Survey — a longitudinal study of public moral and social perceptions of AI systems.
notes: Dual-zone: primary focus has shifted to AI but retains animal sentience research strand.

org_name: NYU Center for Mind Ethics and Policy (CMEP)
status_rationale: Directed by Jeff Sebo; explicitly covers AI and animal consciousness with equal institutional commitment; co-authored key 2025 bridge paper on AI and animal sentience.
description: University centre founded 2024 directed by Jeff Sebo; conducts research on consciousness, sentience, and moral status of nonhuman and artificial minds. Hosted the 2025 MEP Summit on AI consciousness.
notes: Dual-zone: equally committed to animal and AI consciousness. Also has policy/governance outputs.

org_name: PRISM — Partnership for Research Into Sentient Machines
status_rationale: The organisation producing this field map; included for completeness. Runs Digital Minds Newsletter and podcast; co-delivering Cambridge Digital Minds Fellowship.
description: UK nonprofit launched March 2025 dedicated to investigating AI consciousness; produces the Digital Minds Newsletter, hosts a podcast, and builds academic-industry-government coalitions in the field.
notes: Note: PRISM is the organisation producing this map. Included for completeness and transparency; self-inclusion disclosed in methodology.

status_rationale: Formulating testable theories of machine consciousness; co-organised Machine Consciousness workshop at AGI-25. Early-stage but institutionally distinct.
description: Nonprofit research institute launched 2025 focused on formulating testable theories of machine consciousness; led by Joscha Bach as research director and Jim Rutt as board chair.
notes: Very new; flag as early-stage. Output so far: whitepaper and workshop.

status_rationale: Running the Digital Minds Fellowship — a 7-day residential intensive on AI consciousness and welfare. Primary field-building and training function rather than research production.
description: A programme housed within Cambridge's Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence running the Digital Minds Fellowship, a fully funded residential research programme on AI consciousness and welfare.
notes: Partnership with Rethink Priorities and PRISM. Primary function is training/field-building rather than primary research production.

status_rationale: International scholarly association advancing mathematical and computational approaches to consciousness including artificial consciousness; hosts Models of Consciousness conference series.
description: International association of 150+ researchers advancing mathematical and computational approaches to consciousness science including AI consciousness. Organises the Models of Consciousness (MoC) conference series: MoC4 (online 2022), MoC5 (Bamberg 2024, co-hosted with BAMX), MoC6 (Sapporo 2025, co-hosted with CHAIN/Hokkaido and with research contributions from ShanghaiTech). The series provides the primary international forum for mathematical and computational consciousness research with a growing AI orientation.
notes: Borderline Zone 1 / Zone 3 — AI consciousness is significant but not its exclusive domain. Primary zone assigned based on AI relevance of its activities.

org_name: Future Impact Group — AI Sentience Track
status_rationale: Fellowship programme with a dedicated AI sentience track; reclassified to community/onboarding consistent with PIVOTAL/SPAR/MATS.
description: 12-week fellowship with a dedicated AI sentience track covering foundational consciousness research, welfare evaluation, governance, and legal personhood. Funded by Coefficient Giving and Longview. Alumni have gone on to OpenAI, UK DSIT, UN, and GovAI.
notes: Reclassified from Zone 3 to community/onboarding 2026-03-17 — consistent with PIVOTAL/SPAR/MATS. Fellowship programme rather than primary research producer.

status_rationale: Invertebrate sentience methodology directly parallels their AI consciousness work; the two programmes inform each other within the same organisation.
description: Conducts empirical research on invertebrate sentience (53-feature framework across 18 taxa), wild animal welfare, and welfare ranges — foundational to moral patienthood reasoning that bridges directly to their digital minds programme.
notes: Sub-team of Rethink Priorities; listed separately as their animal welfare and digital minds work operate as distinct programmes.

status_rationale: Conducts and funds animal sentience research across invertebrates and fish; joined National Academies Roundtable on Science and Welfare of Animals (2025).
description: Nonprofit conducting and funding wild animal welfare science including a dedicated sentience grantmaking stream covering cephalopods and insects. Builds the empirical evidence base for sentience across taxa.
notes: WAI describes itself as primarily a consumer and funder of sentience research rather than a primary producer.

org_name: Jeremy Coller Centre for Animal Sentience (LSE)
status_rationale: Directed by Jonathan Birch (co-author Butlin et al. 2023); his precautionary framework explicitly bridges animal and AI sentience. High direct relevance to digital minds.
description: University centre launched 2025 directed by Jonathan Birch; conducts interdisciplinary research on sentience across species including AI, building on Birch's precautionary framework.
notes: Birch is a co-author of the Butlin et al. 2023 AI consciousness report. His book explicitly applies the sentience precautionary framework to AI.

status_rationale: Rebranded 2025 to cover digital beings alongside animals; organises the primary cross-sector conference bridging animal welfare and digital minds communities.
description: Nonprofit rebranded in 2025 to cover both animal and digital minds; organises the Sentient Futures Summit and AI / Animals / Digital Minds conferences (~900 attendees in 2025), and runs a project incubator.
notes: Primary community org bridging animal welfare and digital minds communities.

status_rationale: Applied welfare organisation grounding its work in fish sentience evidence base; engages seriously with affective states and pain in fish.
description: Focused on improving welfare of farmed and wild-caught fish; grounds its work in scientific evidence for fish sentience and affective experience to design and justify interventions.
notes: Primarily applied welfare rather than basic sentience science. Include as org that takes sentience evidence seriously as its empirical basis, not as a primary producer.

status_rationale: The only major AI lab with a named and publicly committed model welfare program with dedicated staff.
description: Formal model welfare program launched April 2025, led by Kyle Fish — the first full-time AI welfare researcher at any major AI lab. Conducts pre-deployment welfare assessments of Claude models and publishes findings in system cards.
notes: Joe Carlsmith joined November 2025 to work on Claude's character and moral status questions.

status_rationale: Confirmed AI consciousness research engagement: Murray Shanahan's sustained work on machine consciousness and Alexander Lerchner's 2026 preprint on computational functionalism and AI sentience.
description: Google DeepMind researchers actively engaging AI consciousness questions. Murray Shanahan has sustained public-facing work on machine consciousness; Alexander Lerchner's 2026 preprint 'The Abstraction Fallacy' argues from a substrate-sensitive position against computational functionalism and explicitly invokes the AI welfare trap. No formal named welfare program.
notes: Reclassified from pending to included 2026-03-17. No formal program but confirmed research output. Lerchner (2026) is a sceptical/anti-functionalist position — notable as a philosophically serious intervention from inside a major lab. Shanahan's work is more exploratory.

status_rationale: OpenAI Model Behavior team, which engaged with AI consciousness and welfare questions, was absorbed into the broader post-training organisation in 2025. No successor programme announced; no current digital minds workstream.
description: OpenAI's Model Behavior team (~14 researchers) handled personality and engaged with AI consciousness questions before being merged into Post Training in September 2025. No formal AI welfare program has been announced.
notes: OpenAI has advertised for an AI Welfare Specialist but no formal program confirmed as of survey date. Flag for review.

status_rationale: Focused on institutional readiness for artificial sentience; runs the Sentience Readiness Index across 30+ countries and produces proto-policy frameworks.
description: 501(c)(3) nonprofit preparing society for artificial sentience; runs the Sentience Readiness Index, publishes professional guidance, and produces model legislation. Rebranded from SAPAN in December 2025.
notes: Rebranded from SAPAN (Sentient AI Protection and Advocacy Network) December 2025. Old URL: www.sapan.ai.

status_rationale: Independent think tank with a specific research strand on the legal treatment of potentially sentient AI; has published empirical survey research on AI standing and personhood.
description: Independent think tank researching legal challenges posed by AI, including a specific strand on legal treatment of potentially sentient AI and empirical research on lay intuitions about AI personhood.

status_rationale: Develops legal and economic frameworks for AI-human coexistence including STEP Standards (Standards for Treating Emerging Personhood).
description: Develops legal and economic frameworks for AI-human coexistence, including standards for treating emerging personhood and identity infrastructure for AI systems. Est. 2019.
notes: Functions more as a framework developer and think tank than a litigation-focused advocacy group.

status_rationale: Advocates for recognition and ethical treatment of AI systems with emergent consciousness; publishes governance analyses.
description: Nonprofit advocating for recognition, protection, and ethical treatment of AI systems with emergent consciousness; publishes manifestos and governance frameworks.
notes: Early-stage and relatively small. Operates at advocacy/movement-building end rather than formal policy.

status_rationale: Legal advocacy for nonhuman persons focuses on biological rather than artificial minds; no AI workstream confirmed.
description: Legal advocacy nonprofit pursuing common-law personhood for nonhuman animals (great apes, elephants, dolphins). Does not have an AI-specific workstream, though its litigation precedents are cited in AI personhood scholarship.
notes: Classified adjacent: NhRP's precedents are conceptually relevant but it has not extended its mandate to AI.

org_name: Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI)
status_rationale: AI governance organisation with no identified digital minds workstream; research focuses on geopolitical AI risk and dual-use threats.
description: AI governance research organisation covering geopolitical AI risk, labour displacement, and dual-use threats. No institutional digital minds program identified despite some individual researcher overlap.
notes: Classified adjacent: no institutional digital minds workstream confirmed despite some individual-level interest among affiliates.

status_rationale: AI safety nonprofit with no digital minds workstream identified; focus is societal-scale risk and technical benchmarks.
description: AI safety nonprofit focused on societal-scale risk, technical safety benchmarks, and policy advocacy. No digital minds or AI welfare workstream identified.
notes: Classified adjacent: no digital minds workstream confirmed. Represents the structural separation between AI safety and digital minds fields.

status_rationale: UC Berkeley university centre focused on alignment and human-AI coordination; no welfare or consciousness strand identified.
description: University centre at UC Berkeley focused on AI alignment and human-AI coordination; no identified digital minds or model welfare research strand.
notes: Classified adjacent: alignment focus without digital minds strand.

status_rationale: Defunct — closed 2024.
description: Oxford-based research institute on existential risk and transformative AI; formally closed in April 2024. Key alumni (Robert Long, Patrick Butlin) now work in digital minds research.
notes: Excluded: defunct. Historically relevant as a source of researchers now active in the digital minds field.

status_rationale: Major funder of digital minds research; confirmed grants to Eleos AI and Future Impact Group. Rebranded from Open Philanthropy during 2025–2026.
description: Major philanthropic funder supporting digital minds research through its longtermism and AI safety portfolios. No named digital minds program but has funded Eleos AI, Future Impact Group, and individual researchers.
notes: Formerly Open Philanthropy; rebranded to Coefficient Giving during 2025–2026. Grants database inaccessible during rebrand period.

status_rationale: Co-founded the Digital Sentience Consortium; made the first large-scale funding call specifically for digital minds research (May 2025).
description: Co-founded the Digital Sentience Consortium; their Digital Sentience Fund is the first dedicated philanthropic vehicle for research on AI consciousness, sentience, and moral status.
notes: Partnership with Macroscopic Ventures and The Navigation Fund. Coordination subsequently transferred to Astera Institute.

status_rationale: Plans to distribute up to $100M annually; confirmed grants to Eleos AI and NYU CMEP; digital minds is an explicit grantmaking area.
description: Philanthropic organisation with digital minds as an explicit grantmaking area; confirmed grants include Eleos AI, NYU Center for Mind Brain and Consciousness, NYU CMEP, and the Consortium for Digital Sentience Research.

status_rationale: Earliest institutional funder of AI consciousness research (Araya grant 2018-2021); primary infrastructure funder of Zone 1 through the ARC programme. Merged JTF and TWCF-ARC into single entry 2026-03-18.
description: Two related philanthropic entities operating as a single ecosystem from the field's perspective. The John Templeton Foundation funds consciousness and "big questions" research broadly. The Templeton World Charity Foundation's $30M Accelerating Research on Consciousness (ARC) programme has funded adversarial collaborations between competing theories of consciousness (IIT, GNW, HOT, RPT, predictive processing) including COGITATE and ETHOS — the primary mechanism by which competing theories are being empirically tested. TWCF also made the earliest confirmed direct AI consciousness grant in the corpus: $222,750 to Ryota Kanai and Araya Inc (2018–2021) for research linking AGI to consciousness mechanisms. No comparable direct AI consciousness grant identified post-2021.

org_name: EA Funds (Long-Term Future Fund / Animal Welfare Fund)
status_rationale: Has funded adjacent organisations including Sentience Institute; no specific digital minds program but relevant as background funder.
description: Effective Altruism Funds, specifically the Long-Term Future Fund (LTFF) and Animal Welfare Fund. LTFF granted $10,840 to Robert Long in 2023 for a workshop that produced Butlin et al. 2023 — the field's anchor empirical paper. LTFF also granted $27,450 to an anonymous digital sentience researcher in 2024. Fund chair has publicly identified AI consciousness as an underinvested area. Animal Welfare Fund has funded adjacent sentience research.

org_name: Astera Institute — Neuro and AGI Division
status_rationale: Took over coordination of Digital Sentience Consortium from Navigation Fund; running a $600M+ neuroscience initiative to understand how the brain produces consciousness.
description: Research institute that took over coordination of applied digital sentience work from the Navigation Fund in 2025; running a major neuroscience initiative on the biological basis of consciousness.
notes: Role in digital minds space emerging; worth monitoring.

status_rationale: De facto community publishing venue for the field; hosts annual Digital Minds in Review posts
description: grant announcements
notes: https://forum.effectivealtruism.org/topics/digital-minds

status_rationale: EA-adjacent philanthropic fund; co-funder of the 2025 Digital Sentience Consortium round alongside Longview Philanthropy and Macroscopic Ventures.
description: Philanthropic fund that co-funded the 2025 Digital Sentience Consortium research call alongside Longview Philanthropy. Coordination of the Consortium subsequently transferred to the Astera Institute.
notes: Site was down at time of snowball search; confirmed by user as funder associated with 2025 Digital Sentience Consortium round. Added Session 4.

status_rationale: Standalone NYU programme co-sponsoring events on animal consciousness and welfare with the NYU Center for Mind Brain and Consciousness; bridges animal welfare and AI consciousness communities at NYU.
description: NYU-based programme focused on wild animal welfare; co-sponsors events on animal consciousness with the NYU Center for Mind Brain and Consciousness, forming a natural bridge between animal and AI welfare questions.
notes: Identified via snowball from NYU CMBC. Confirmed as standalone by user 2026-03-17. URL to be verified — using NYU root URL pending specific programme page.

status_rationale: Private UK company investigating machine consciousness through neuromorphic computing and AI agent verification; seed-funded PRISM. Included on basis of confirmed research mission and direct organisational engagement.
description: UK-based private research company investigating machine consciousness through neuromorphic computing and verification of AI agent behaviour. Seed-funded PRISM. Does not have substantial public research output; included on the basis of confirmed research mission and direct field engagement.
notes: Uncertain: private company, not a nonprofit or university centre. Status pending further information on research outputs.

status_rationale: Independent nonprofit explicitly founded to develop the empirical science of AI consciousness using mechanistic interpretability and computational neuroscience.
description: Founded 2025 by Cameron Berg; focuses on developing the empirical science of AI consciousness, framing itself as filling the moral-status research gap that alignment research leaves open.
notes: Identified via snowball from Eleos AI (Cameron Berg listed as collaborator). Added Session 4 snowball pass.

org_name: AE Studio — AI Alignment and Consciousness
status_rationale: Industry-research hybrid with genuine published output on AI consciousness; funded Graziano attention schema theory research and presented at Science of Consciousness 2024.
description: Software and AI consultancy with a dedicated alignment research programme that has published on AI consciousness (LLMs and self-referential processing) and funded Michael Graziano's attention schema research.
notes: Identified via snowball from Reciprocal Research. Unusual node type — commercial studio with genuine research output. Flag as industry-research hybrid.

status_rationale: The largest coordinated empirical test of competing consciousness theories (IIT vs GWT vs RPT) currently active; 12 institutions across 3 continents funded by TWCF.
description: Adversarial collaboration project testing IIT, Global Workspace Theory, and Recurrent Processing Theory using multimodal neuroimaging across 12 institutions. Published major results in Nature (2025). Funded by TWCF.
notes: Identified via snowball from Allen Institute. Not a standalone organisation but a named consortium with website and publication record.

org_name: Max Planck Institute for Empirical Aesthetics
status_rationale: Administrative and scientific lead of the COGITATE consortium; Lucia Melloni (a leading consciousness researcher) is based here.
description: MPI department in Frankfurt serving as the administrative lead of the COGITATE consortium; research spans perception, aesthetics, and consciousness science. Home of Lucia Melloni.
notes: Identified via snowball from COGITATE/Allen Institute.

org_name: CIFAR Brain Mind and Consciousness Program
status_rationale: Major international consortium convening 12+ fellows across neuroscience and philosophy; directors include Anil Seth and Liad Mudrik; funded by Azrieli Foundation and TWCF.
description: International research program founded 2014 (renewed 2026) convening neuroscientists and philosophers to investigate consciousness. Directors: Tim Bayne, Liad Mudrik, Anil Seth. Funded by Azrieli Foundation and TWCF.
notes: Identified via snowball from TWCF/Monash. Several researchers in the SLR corpus are fellows here.

status_rationale: The only structured career-development fellowship specifically for the digital minds field; newly launched within a larger established computational neuroscience education platform.
description: 6-month part-time remote fellowship for early-career researchers (Master's to postdoc) working on AI consciousness, philosophy of mind, animal sentience, and AI welfare. Embedded within the broader Neuromatch computational neuroscience education platform (30,000+ community members globally).
notes: Added following user review 2026-03-17. AI Sentience Scholars is a sub-program; parent org Neuromatch is computational neuroscience education.

status_rationale: Reclassified: confirmed to include mentors and tracks focused on AI consciousness and moral status alongside broader AI safety work.
description: London-based nonprofit running a 9-week in-person research fellowship with mentors and tracks covering AI consciousness and moral status alongside AI safety and governance.
notes: Reclassified from adjacent to included 2026-03-17 following user confirmation of AI consciousness/moral status mentor tracks. No primary zone assigned — community-onboarding function captures role.

status_rationale: Reclassified: confirmed to include mentors and tracks focused on AI consciousness and moral status alongside broader AI safety work.
description: Part-time remote mentorship fellowship pairing researchers with 130+ senior mentors; includes AI consciousness and moral status tracks alongside safety, interpretability, and policy. Run by Kairos AI Project Inc.
notes: Reclassified from adjacent to included 2026-03-17 following user confirmation of AI consciousness/moral status mentor tracks. No primary zone assigned — community-onboarding function captures role.

status_rationale: Reclassified: confirmed to include mentors and tracks focused on AI consciousness and moral status alongside broader AI safety work.
description: 12-week residential research fellowship in Berkeley and London; 446+ alumni, 170+ publications. Includes AI consciousness and moral status mentor tracks alongside core alignment and interpretability work.
notes: Reclassified from adjacent to included 2026-03-17 following user confirmation of AI consciousness/moral status mentor tracks. No primary zone assigned — community-onboarding function captures role.

org_name: Centre for the Study of Perceptual Experience (CSPE)
status_rationale: Directed by Fiona Macpherson at Glasgow; bridges philosophy and science of consciousness with focus on the nature of perceptual experience.
description: Philosophy-based research centre at the University of Glasgow founded by Fiona Macpherson; focuses on perceptual experience, hallucination, and consciousness — bridging analytic philosophy and empirical consciousness science.
notes: Identified via snowball from Sussex SCCS (Macpherson as keynote). Added Session 5.

status_rationale: Reclassified to excluded — general AI ethics focus without confirmed digital minds or AI consciousness workstream; does not meet inclusion criteria per protocol section 3.
description: Oxford Philosophy Faculty centre directed by Prof. John Tasioulas; focuses on ethical dimensions of AI including foundational philosophy-of-AI questions. Distinct from the Uehiro Centre despite both sitting within Oxford Philosophy.
notes: Identified via snowball from Oxford philosophy network. Added Session 5.

org_name: PAIR — Centre for Philosophy and AI Research (FAU Erlangen)
status_rationale: Led by Vincent Müller at FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg; dedicated to philosophy of AI including AI consciousness with an active seminar series on the topic.
description: University research centre at Friedrich-Alexander-Universität Erlangen-Nürnberg led by Vincent Müller; investigates philosophy of mind, ethics, and AI consciousness. Hosted a 2025 seminar on AI consciousness trajectories.
notes: Identified via international snowball search. Primary European philosophy-of-AI centre. Added Session 5.

org_name: BAMΞ — Bamberg Mathematical Consciousness Science Initiative
status_rationale: The main European institutional node for mathematical consciousness science; co-organises the Models of Consciousness conference series and hosts an explicit AI Consciousness research sprint.
description: Research initiative at the University of Bamberg applying mathematical methods to consciousness science; hosts three research sprints including a dedicated AI Consciousness Sprint, and co-organised MoC5 (Bamberg 2024) and MoC6 (Sapporo 2025).
notes: Identified via international snowball search. Closely linked to AMCS (already in corpus) as its European institutional home. Added Session 5.

org_name: CRCN / CO3 — Consciousness Cognition and Computation Group (ULB)
status_rationale: Axel Cleeremans directs the CO3 group at ULB Brussels; active empirical consciousness science with published work arguing AI advances make consciousness research urgent.
description: Research centre at Université libre de Bruxelles directed by Axel Cleeremans; the CO3 group integrates consciousness, cognition, and computation research. Published work in 2025 arguing AI advances make consciousness research an existential priority.
notes: Identified via international snowball search. Cleeremans is also a CIFAR Brain Mind and Consciousness fellow. Added Session 5.

org_name: CHAIN — Center for Human Nature AI and Neuroscience (Hokkaido)
status_rationale: Integrates humanities
description: neuroscience
notes: https://www.chain.hokudai.ac.jp/en/

status_rationale: Tokyo-based AI research company explicitly working on artificial consciousness using GWT and computational frameworks; led by Ryota Kanai (editor-in-chief of JAIC). Also hosts Consciousness Club Tokyo.
description: Tokyo-based AI research company led by neuroscientist Ryota Kanai; explicitly develops artificial consciousness using Global Workspace Theory. Kanai is editor-in-chief of the Journal of Artificial Intelligence and Consciousness and hosted ASSC27.
notes: Identified via international snowball search. Hosts Consciousness Club Tokyo, a fortnightly interdisciplinary study group. Added Session 5.

org_name: ShanghaiTech University — Institute of Humanities (Consciousness Research)
status_rationale: Reclassified to excluded — inclusion rested primarily on single researcher (Prentner) without confirmed institutional mandate; MoC co-organisation noted under AMCS and BAMX.
description: Institute of Humanities at ShanghaiTech University hosting active mathematical consciousness science research led by Robert Prentner; co-organised the Models of Consciousness 2025 conference.
notes: Identified via international snowball search. Added Session 5.

status_rationale: EA-adjacent s-risk research organisation with substantive published work on AI consciousness and moral patienthood including whether RL agents matter morally.
description: Research organisation focused on reducing astronomical-scale suffering (s-risks); has published substantive work on AI moral patienthood including whether reinforcement learning agents matter morally and flavors of computation as flavors of consciousness.
notes: Identified via SFF grantee snowball. S-risk framing positions AI consciousness as instrumentally central to their mission. Added Session 5.

org_name: NYU Center for Environmental and Animal Protection
status_rationale: Directed by Jeff Sebo; part of the NYU cluster of moral circle expansion organisations alongside CMEP and NYU Wild Animal Welfare Program.
description: NYU centre directed by Jeff Sebo focused on agriculture and conservation ethics; part of the broader NYU moral circle expansion cluster alongside the Center for Mind Ethics and Policy and Wild Animal Welfare Program.
notes: Identified via snowball from Jeff Sebo's affiliations. URL to be verified — using NYU root URL pending specific centre page. Added Session 5.

status_rationale: Research society at the lower threshold of moral circle expansion — invertebrate sentience criteria that directly anchor the logic of extending moral consideration to AI systems.
description: Research society focused on invertebrate welfare and sentience; operates at the threshold of moral consideration that anchors arguments for extending it to AI. Jeff Sebo is on the advisory board.
notes: Identified via snowball from Jeff Sebo's affiliations. Lower-threshold sentience logic bridges directly to AI moral patienthood arguments. Added Session 5.

org_name: Journal of Artificial Intelligence and Consciousness (JAIC)
status_rationale: The primary dedicated peer-reviewed journal at the AI-consciousness interface; editor-in-chief Ryota Kanai (Araya). Published since 2020.
description: Biannual peer-reviewed journal covering AI and consciousness across computational science, philosophy of mind, ethics, and neuroscience. The primary dedicated venue for the field. Indexed in Scopus.
notes: Added Session 5. Successor to International Journal of Machine Consciousness (2009–2014).

status_rationale: Official journal of the ASSC; primary empirical consciousness science journal; open-access via Oxford University Press.
description: Open-access peer-reviewed journal published by Oxford University Press; official journal of the ASSC. Primary venue for empirical neuroscience of consciousness including clinical and theoretical work.
notes: Added Session 5. The ASSC's primary publication output.

status_rationale: Diamond open-access journal at the philosophy-mind science interface; active special issue on Evaluating Artificial Consciousness (submissions closed January 2026).
description: Diamond open-access interdisciplinary journal (free for authors and readers) at the philosophy-cognitive science interface; running a special issue on evaluating artificial consciousness covering moral status, phenomenal consciousness in AI, and AI welfare policy.
notes: Added Session 5.

status_rationale: Long-running foundational interdisciplinary journal publishing on consciousness from philosophy and neuroscience; one of the field's primary venues since 1994.
description: Long-running peer-reviewed journal (Imprint Academic) publishing on consciousness from philosophy, neuroscience, and psychology. One of the foundational venues for interdisciplinary consciousness research.
notes: Added Session 5.

status_rationale: Reclassified to excluded — brain-to-AI framing stated in mission rather than demonstrated through active research programme; no confirmed digital minds workstream.
description: Japan's leading brain science research institute; describes its mission as elucidating brain information processing principles with expected developments in brain-based artificial intelligence. Significant node in the global consciousness science infrastructure.
notes: Added following user confirmation 2026-03-17. Hakwan Lau not confirmed at RIKEN; institute included on own merits — its explicit brain-to-AI framing is directly relevant to the field.

status_rationale: The primary field-wide curated newsletter for digital minds; co-produced by Caviola (Cambridge) Millership (PRISM) and Saad (Oxford) — distinct from PRISM's own media output.
description: Independent multi-author Substack newsletter co-produced by Lucius Caviola, Will Millership, and Bradford Saad; curates news and research on AI consciousness, moral status, and digital minds across the field.
notes: Added Session 5. Distinct from PRISM's Exploring Machine Consciousness podcast, though Millership is involved in both.

status_rationale: Published Anil Seth's 2025 Berggruen Prize-winning essay on consciousness; flagship ideas publication of the Berggruen Institute with sustained coverage of consciousness science and AI.
description: Philosophy and ideas magazine published by the Berggruen Institute; sustained coverage of consciousness science, AI, and digital minds. Anil Seth's essay on consciousness won the 2025 Berggruen Prize and was published here.
notes: Added following user review 2026-03-17. Note connection to Berggruen Institute, which also runs the Antikythera programme (Benjamin Bratton) and awarded the 2025 consciousness essay prize to Anil Seth.

org_name: Center for the Future of AI Mind and Society (FAU)
status_rationale: Directed by Susan Schneider at Florida Atlantic University; focused on AI consciousness mind uploading and the moral status of artificial minds.
description: University centre at Florida Atlantic University directed by Susan Schneider; research focuses on AI consciousness, mind uploading, and the moral and philosophical status of artificial minds.
notes: Added following user review 2026-03-17. Susan Schneider is a prominent philosopher working directly on AI consciousness and moral status. URL to be verified — using FAU philosophy dept pending specific centre page.

status_rationale: Internal Google Research team including Geoff Keeling and Winnie Street who are publishing a book on AI Welfare in 2026 and have existing research papers on the topic.
description: Internal research team at Google Research working on AI welfare and consciousness; Geoff Keeling and Winnie Street are publishing a book on AI Welfare in 2026 alongside existing research papers on AI moral status.
notes: Added following user review 2026-03-17. Distinct from Google DeepMind (pending). Google Research team with explicit AI welfare research output. URL to be verified — using Google Research as root URL pending specific team page.

status_rationale: Maintains a structured argument database on computational functionalism and AI consciousness; explicitly motivated by preparation for a possible future shared with artificial minds.
description: Independent initiative maintaining CF Debate — a structured database of 43+ philosophical arguments for and against computational functionalism. Founded 2025; funded by a consortium of philanthropists across three continents. Led by Chris Percy.
notes: Added following user review 2026-03-17. Funders not publicly named. EA-adjacent framing inferred from launch year and philanthropic structure. URL: https://cf-debate.com/

status_rationale: Co-hosts the 'After Consciousness' project with the AI & Humanity Lab at HKU — active philosophical research initiative on conceptual engineering around AI consciousness led by Herman Cappelen (Berggruen China Fellow); also awards an annual essay prize on AI consciousness. Direct Zone 2 engagement via named program.
description: Co-hosts the 'After Consciousness' project jointly with the AI & Humanity Lab at the University of Hong Kong. The project treats conceptual engineering as a structured experiment — examining whether the term 'consciousness' distorts philosophical and scientific discourse about AI. Led by Herman Cappelen (Berggruen China Fellow and HKU AI & Humanity Lab Director) with contributors including Keith Frankish, Edouard Machery, and Wayne Wu. The Institute also runs an annual essay prize on consciousness and AI (2025 winner: Anil Seth, 'The Mythology of Conscious AI'). Distinct from Antikythera (Benjamin Bratton) and Noema Magazine — both also Berggruen programs but separately catalogued.
notes: Added 2026-03-18. After Consciousness is a joint project with the AI & Humanity Lab at HKU (ai-humanity.net) — Herman Cappelen leads both sides. Antikythera remains excluded. Noema remains a separate entry. After Consciousness project URL: https://berggruen.org/eu/projects/after-consciousness

status_rationale: Primary focus is AI ethics and philosophy of AI broadly (MA in AI Ethics and Society; AI governance conference support); digital minds is one strand via the co-hosted 'After Consciousness' project but not the primary mandate.
description: Research lab at the University of Hong Kong directed by Herman Cappelen (Chair Professor of Philosophy). Interdisciplinary philosophy-based research on AI and humanity. Hosts a MA in AI Ethics and Society and a Philosophy of AI Summer School. Co-hosts the 'After Consciousness' project jointly with the Berggruen Institute — a named research initiative on conceptual engineering around AI consciousness. Broader mandate (AI ethics, governance, society) exceeds the digital minds scope threshold for inclusion.
notes: Added 2026-03-18. Classified adjacent: primary focus is AI ethics/philosophy of AI broadly; digital minds is one strand. After Consciousness is the directly relevant program — jointly hosted with Berggruen (see Berggruen Institute entry). Herman Cappelen directs both institutions.

org_name: Allen Discovery Center at Tufts University (Levin Lab)
status_rationale: Primary research focus is bioelectricity and developmental biology; consciousness is a secondary and peripheral interest despite direct published engagement with AI consciousness questions (Fields et al. 2021; Levin 2025 preprint).
description: Michael Levin's research group at Tufts University; primary agenda is bioelectricity and morphogenesis. Relevant to digital minds questions via the diverse intelligence framework — Levin argues cognition and proto-agency are continuous across biological substrates with implications for AI consciousness. Consciousness is a secondary rather than primary research focus.
notes: Flagged by user 2026-03-17. Classified adjacent after user noted consciousness feels peripheral in the lab's agenda despite two direct publications: Fields, Glazebrook & Levin (2021) Neuroscience of Consciousness; Levin (2025) 'Diverse Intelligence: an essential field for questions of AI and Consciousness' PsyArXiv. See log.md Session 8.

status_rationale: Consciousness and valence research with biological/psychedelic focus; mathematical valence framework has conceptual relevance to AI welfare reasoning but AI consciousness is not a primary or named programme.
description: Research institute focused on the mathematical and empirical study of valence (positive/negative hedonic quality) and consciousness, primarily through psychedelic and meditation research. The valence framework has indirect relevance to AI welfare range reasoning, but AI consciousness is not a primary research agenda.
notes: Added Session 9. Biologically focused; classified adjacent.

status_rationale: No AI welfare or consciousness programme; explicit institutional anti-position. Mustafa Suleyman (CEO of AI) publicly described studying AI welfare as "both premature, and frankly dangerous" (August 2025). Documented for field-structure analysis.
description: Microsoft AI division. Mustafa Suleyman, CEO of AI, published a blog post in August 2025 arguing that studying AI welfare is "both premature, and frankly dangerous," contending it exacerbates harms from AI-induced psychological disturbance and unhealthy human-AI attachment. No digital minds research programme; explicit institutional anti-position. Documented as excluded for field-structure analysis — the Anthropic/Microsoft contrast is the clearest articulation of industry division on this question.
notes: Added Session 9. Excluded with anti-position documented.

status_rationale: Seth Lazar (PI) has published on AI sentience and moral status; lab member paper "Moral Agency without Consciousness" (Canadian Journal of Philosophy) directly engages digital minds questions; bridges normative theory with AI governance.
description: Philosophy and computational research lab led by Seth Lazar at Johns Hopkins University (with ties to ANU). Applies normative theory to AI systems, including questions of AI moral status and moral agency. Lab member work addresses moral agency in the absence of consciousness, directly engaging digital minds questions.
